DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (through Court Call):
Andrew Stroud, Esq. 916-491-3050 of Hanson Bridgett LLP for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Jonathan B. Nelson.
Michael Carroll, Esq. 925-283-6641 of Carroll Law Offices for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Brendan McGrath.
Sheena B. Patel, Esq. 510-433-2600 x2647 of Rankin Shuey Ranucci Mintz Lampasona & Reynolds for Defendant DeClan O'Donnell dba Crana Plastering & Stucco.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views and inquires which part of the tentative ruling is contested. Parties submit to the tentative ruling except for the provision on the joinder. Atty. Stroud argues why the joinder is necessary. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT JONATHAN NELSON's DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling except for who is joining.

The Denurrer is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend. No present controversy for which no remedy at law is available has been asserted. The demurer to the causes of action for trespass and encroachment re: standing is overruled. While the moving party cited several cases with favorable language/holdings, none of those cases involved similar facts, i.e. a plaintiff currently out-of-possession seeking damages for trespass/encroachment while in possession. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to join current owners of the property. Any determination in this action regarding the alleged encroachment, which is permanent in nature, implicates the rights of the current owners as is asserted in the Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("Such encroachments substantially diminish the value of the McGrath Property and interferes with the quiet enjoyment by the homeowner and any subsequent owner of the McGrath Property." SAXC, paragraph 45)

Mr. Carroll to submit a proposed order that tracks the tentative ruling including the provision on the joinder but leave a blank space as to which party is joining in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. =(501/REQ)
